ChIJDzX2d3x2bjQRha-HOsf7LIA.formatted_address: No. 1號, Heyi Rd, West Central District, Tainan City, Taiwan 700
ChIJDzX2d3x2bjQRha-HOsf7LIA.editorial_summary: Trendy rooms & suites in a modern property with 2 restaurants, 2 bars & an outdoor pool.

text.text: This our second Silks experience! Top level service. We only stayed one night, but they held our luggage for several hours before check in and again on the day we checked out. Breakfast buffet was great for both western and Taiwanese patrons. Spacious rooms. Great comfy bathtub! LOVE this place!

text.text: Sleek, luxury 5-star hotel with excellent service and great facilities near the Confucius temple. When you check-in, they welcome you with winter melon tea, which was sweet and refreshing. We had the deluxe family queen bedroom, which was super spacious, with two queen-size beds, a long sofa and a dining table area. We were fortunate to be on the higher floors, and had a good view of the city looking South. The bathroom was large with a separate tub and shower, which was nice. The beds were a tiny bit hard but we slept fine. One highlight of the room is the amenities. The hotel provides an array of teas, coffees, cold drinks and snacks, not to mention extra bottles of water plus a water pitcher. (There are water dispensers on every floor.) There’s plenty of towels, a rare thing for hotels these days, plus slippers and bathrobes. Everything made our stay extremely comfortable and we enjoyed every minute here. The only downside of the hotel is that it’s also a popular wedding banquet venue, so the lobby gets crammed up with loud banquet guests, who like to gather and smoke right outside the front entrance, making it unpleasant to get in and out while they’re there. The hotel also shares outdoor spaces with nearby department stores, such as parking entrances, so quite a lot of speeding cars will cross paths with hotel foot traffic, making it dangerous to walk around. But overall, it was a lovely experience, and we would make this our go-to stay in Tainan anytime we visit.

text.text:
Our hotel room was very spacious and nice. Bathroom large and open. Shower water pressure is nice and hot. Room was clean and bed was comfortable. The welcome amenities were great! Came with face masks, snacks and free tea in the mini fridge. The hotel is eco friendly so they provide a water jug and there’s a water refill station on every floor.

Even though we didn’t get to use them, if you stay at the hotel, there are discount vouchers that you can apply towards the restaurants in the hotel too.

Location is pretty great. 30 min drive from the Tainan HSR station and about a 15-20 minute walk to old town Tainan market.

Overall really enjoyed our stay here and would be happy to revisit.

text.text: Very convenient location with an adjoined shopping center that has interesting shops featuring mainly Japanese stores. The hotel has in building/basement parking B5-B7 that is complimentary to the stay which is very helpful as the streets in Tainan are quite small and it’s tricky to navigate. Within walking distance there are a lot of dining options and some interesting things to look at. The hotel breakfast has two options - we chose the Chinese version which is a Hongkong style breakfast with dimsum, roast duck and other cooked food in the buffet. If you are a vegetarian they accommodate and will make items for you in addition to items already out flagged with a green card on the buffet table. And you barista your own half coffee/half tea yinyang drink. A bit of arts and craft drink mixology on your part. It’s pretty fun and breaks up the monotony of Taiwanese breakfast. The room is super spacious and what I appreciate is the multi function large room dividers that act as an additional sound barrier at night when you sleep. There could be a lot of noise in the hotel hallway in the evening as guests come in and out but that large barrier seals off any outside distractions/disturbances. I had a good night sleep and I would stay here again. It’s very comfortable. On the way back from breakfast - I looked at the pool and it’s an outside rooftop pool at level 4. And there’s an adjoining gym with equipment and weights that’s staffed. That’s impressive.

text.text: The Silks Place is perfectly located, placing you right at the center of everything. I was delighted to be upgraded to a top-floor suite with a cozy Japanese seating area, perfect for enjoying tea. The hotel was incredibly generous, offering complimentary sparkling tea. Beyond that, the whole ambiance stood out: the lobby felt grand with its rich Chinese art style, and seeing so many small Chinese art exhibits made the experience truly unforgettable.

ChIJcWJbO3x3bjQRw0psRJrHKUY.formatted_address: No. 28號, Chenggong Rd, North District, Tainan City, Taiwan 704003
ChIJcWJbO3x3bjQRw0psRJrHKUY.editorial_summary: Upscale hotel offering a coffee shop, 2 restaurants & a gym, plus a rooftop terrace with a pool.

text.text:
A lovely hotel located in the center of Tainan. It’s a large hotel with very friendly staff and plenty of facilities.

There is a swimming pool. Although it’s small and more suitable for a relaxing dip than serious swimming, the water is clean and perfect for someone like me who isn’t very confident in the water. The hotel also has a gym and a self-service laundry area with affordable coin-operated machines.

Bicycles are available for free, which made it very easy for us to explore Tainan. The breakfast buffet is impressive, offering a wide variety of dishes.

It’s great value for money and conveniently located not far from the train station, though not the high-speed rail station. They are also very eco-conscious, providing refillable water jars instead of bottled water, which I think is a nice touch.

Will definitely stay here again!

text.text:
The front desk staff were incredibly helpful. We showed them our reservation, and they kindly held our luggage until check-in, which gave us the freedom to go explore Tainan for the day without carrying bags around. It was such a relief knowing our belongings were secure.

The hotel itself is very nice—spotlessly clean, bright, and well-lit throughout. We stayed between Christmas and New Year’s, and the Christmas decorations were a lovely touch. Coming from the U.S., we didn’t see much Christmas décor around Taiwan, so this felt like a special and unexpected treat.

Breakfast was included in the package we chose, and it’s definitely something worth mentioning—it was very well done and exceeded our expectations. The rooms were modern, extremely clean, and comfortable. No complaints from the boss (aka my wife), which says a lot.
We would absolutely stay here again on our next visit to Tainan. Highly recommended!

ChIJqfAvaGJ2bjQR678EbZXaqfE.editorial_summary: Seafood, soup & other traditional offerings lie in store at this buzzy, banquet-style restaurant.

text.text:
Reservations required — consider booking two days in advance to get seats. If you are unprepared (as I was), you can also order take-out.

The sticky rice with mud crab is the dish to order here. It’s delicious. Order it! The crabs are a little small so consider sizing up. Other dishes are decent, but the sticky rice with mud crab is the main item people come here for, so don’t leave without it.

Atmosphere is very nice but I didn’t really get a chance to enjoy it given that I did take-out.

text.text:
A friend recommended this well known traditional Taiwanese restaurant.

Food: 4/5 - we enjoyed the steamed fresh prawns and chicken herbal soup. I was disappointed with the steamed mud crab with sticky rice. The crab was small and didn’t have much meat for that particular crab. The herbal soup was flavorful and tasty. Something my mom used to make.

Service: 3/5 - lots of servers standing around but not as attentive. We had a bottle of sake & ended up serving ourselves.

Atmosphere: 4/5 - bright and clean inside. They had fresh Easter lilies in vases. If you don’t like the scent as is, it may affect your meal.

text.text:
Had a bizarre experience here. The photos online looked great. However, at 5:15pm they had run out of much of what we were hoping to order - pork sausage, monkfish, etc. The pork knuckles were not what you would expect from braised pork knuckles elsewhere. They were fried cutlets. Room temperature. The replacement fish they gave us was COLDer than room temperature. It was also very tough. As if they had overcooked it during lunch then let it sit out all day. That’s when I noticed a ton of food was sitting out cafeteria style, not made fresh.

When my friend and I entered, the host made a big commotion to get us seated. Unsure if it’s because we are obviously foreigners. Everyone in the restaurant was staring at us. Then a similar spectacle happened when she was trying to explain what food they were out of. Then again when we tried to leave, she was yelling at us about the dried, cold fish I had left on the table asking if we wanted a to-go box. I did not. She wasn’t yelling angrily, it was just embarrassing to have the whole restaurant staring at us every time she spoke.

Overall, very expensive, confusing, and disappointing. Surprised Michelin would recommend this place.

There’s also chili sauce available, which makes it taste even better! I recommend adding an egg to the rice cake (a little sauce), it’s a perfect match.

The rice with pork is also delicious! The spices are very aromatic, the pork is tender, and the shredded pork topping adds a nice flavor and texture.

The place is clean and comfortable, with safe parking for bicycles and motorcycles, though parking for cars may be limited. The staff are friendly and even recommended their best dishes. I came here because this shop received a Michelin Star, and it truly lives up to its reputation, the food is amazing!

text.text:
Since the first generation, the zongzi have shrunk a bit. Perhaps because people don't do heavy lifting anymore, the portion sizes have been adjusted a bit. The taste is just as good as always, but the supply is limited right now, so you really need to get yours early.

The vegetable zongzi doesn't have peanut powder, but it's packed with peanuts. Combined with the fragrant moon peach leaves and the soft, chewy glutinous rice, it's truly one heck of a mouthful. The shop offers complimentary barley tea to help with the glutinous rice, but I highly recommend ordering the miso soup. The generous amount of dried fish is truly worth it.

originalText.text:
從第一代吃到現在，粽子有縮小一點了，可能現在人不作粗活，份量調整了一些些。味道一如往常，只是現在數量有限，真的要早早來搶購。
菜粽是沒有加花生粉的，但已包滿好好多的花生，加上月桃粽葉香氣，軟Q的糯米，真的一口接一口，店家有提供免費的麥茶解膩也防粽子的胃脹，但強烈建議點一碗味噌湯，那滿滿的小魚乾，真的物超所值。

text.text: The rice dumplings were too wet and sticky, and there were quite a few peanuts in each dumpling. Adding the chili sauce from the table would have improved the flavor. The miso soup was just average, nothing special. Communication and service could be improved.

text.text:
I had the pleasure to discover this restaurant in Hualien one year ago. I loved the dishes so much that I had no other choice to go to the newly opened place in Tainan.
Without surprise, everything was excellent: the very kind service, the flavor of the dishes which are a delicate fusion between Japanese and Western cuisine (try the Japanese style hamburger with the demi-glace sauce or the chicken breast with different types of sauces) and the atmosphere of the place (cosy and friendly with vintage Japanese music) that makes you want to stay till the late evening.
The menu set, at a very reasonable price, allows you to try different side dishes that are all delicious as well.
Besides, if you come to Tainan by train, the restaurant is just near the train station, which is quite convenient.

text.text:
The burger patty was perfectly seared on the outside, and the meat inside was firm and juicy. I absolutely loved their scallion and salt sauce! The homemade scallion and salt sauce had a satisfying crunch of onions, a perfect balance with the savory burger patty 😋

Each set meal includes two homemade side dishes, all very refreshing 👍. The rice portion is adjustable; I recommend the "more rice" option (no extra charge) for those with a larger appetite, as the burger patty is so delicious and goes perfectly with rice 😆!

This time I also ordered the onion tamagoyaki (with cheese inside), which was perfectly cooked and tender. The onion topping added another layer of flavor, a perfect finishing touch ✨

I felt incredibly satisfied after eating~ I'll definitely come back next time 🥰

originalText.text:
漢堡排表皮煎得微焦，裡面的肉質紮實又多汁，真的好喜歡這家的蔥鹽醬口味！店家自製的蔥鹽醬吃得到洋蔥脆脆的口感，跟鹹香的漢堡排達到了完美平衡😋

每份套餐可以選兩樣自製小菜，口味都很清爽👍白飯份量可以調整，建議食量大的人選「飯多」（無需加價），因為漢堡排真的太美味、太下飯了😆！

這次還加點了洋蔥泥玉子燒（裡面還有起司），口感煎得很軟嫩，上面的洋蔥泥讓口味更有層次，很有畫龍點睛的效果✨

吃完覺得超級滿足～下次會再來🥰

text.text: The restaurant is tucked away in a small alley next to a bustling main road; after going down a small slope, you push open the door to enter a small courtyard. The interior has a minimalist Japanese style. I sat at the bar that day and ordered the #RichRedWineMushroomBurgerSteakSet, which included two side dishes of your choice. Having tried the burger steak at Manatsu, I was deeply impressed by its thick, substantial, and juicy texture. The perfect balance of lean and fat meat was so satisfying! It's currently my number one Japanese burger steak. 🫶🏻

originalText.text: 店子隱於熱鬧大路旁的小巷，下小坡後推門進小庭園。用餐室內是日系簡約風，當日坐吧台，點了#香醇紅酒蘑菇漢堡排套餐，可自選2個小菜。吃過真夏的漢堡排就被厚、實在、噴汁深深吸引，肥瘦肉適中，好滿足～暫是心中第一位的日式漢堡排🫶🏻

The sundries and snacks were all very attractive, and there was even a Dazaifu Tenmangu amulet. The woman who managed the shop was very friendly and offered simple drinks like soft serve ice cream and coffee, but these were only available outside.

Originally a long-abandoned hair salon, Kuwahara Shoten has completely recreated this Showa-era general store, meticulously detailing every detail. It's no wonder so many people come to take photos. It's truly remarkable how the old wooden structure and rusted iron sheets can be rendered so beautifully.

text.text:
We visited the original building of the Tainan Art Museum during the Lunar New Year period and thoroughly enjoyed it.

The architecture alone is worth seeing — elegant, well-proportioned and full of quiet character. The restored historic façade gives it real presence, and inside the galleries feel calm, airy and thoughtfully laid out.

The collection itself is compact but engaging, with a strong focus on modern Taiwanese art. During our visit there was a noticeable emphasis on Chinese New Year themes, particularly celebrating the upcoming Year of the Horse (2026). Rather than feeling decorative, the festive elements were integrated into contemporary works in a way that felt respectful and creative.

It’s not a vast museum, but that’s part of the appeal — you can take your time and properly absorb what’s on display without feeling rushed.

A very worthwhile cultural stop in Tainan, especially when paired with the nearby temples and historic sites. Thoughtful, well curated and full of personality.

Having said that, there is not much room for exhibits, forcing the bigger pieces to be housed in what seems to be a later-built extension area. If I am being truly honest, this building is skippable if you are low on time. Otherwise, since the ticket for each of the galleries is also usable for the other, it might be fun for a change of scenery.

7/10 decent enough

text.text:
We visited this bar on our second night in Tainan and were met with an unexpected sense of ease. The owner-bartender welcomed us in English the moment we stepped in, even remembering the note about our anniversary. He checked in just enough, sharing small parts of the bar’s story - the way each element comes together through collaborations with designers, photographers, and roasters.

The space itself was warm and modern, the kind of room that feels thoughtfully put together without trying too hard. At some point, I realised I felt unusually at home for a place I had never been to. Maybe it was the atmosphere, maybe it was the way we were looked after.

By the time we left, what stayed with me wasn’t just the visit but the quiet feeling of belonging the place managed to create.

text.text:
We had heard good things about Moonrock Bar, and while the drinks themselves were good, the overall experience didn’t quite live up to other bars we’ve visited in Tainan.

The atmosphere felt a bit rigid and lacking warmth, technically fine, but missing that sense of soul or ease that makes you want to stay longer.

Booking was also frustrating. We called to reserve for a group of 8 on a Monday night and were told the maximum was 6, and that separate tables weren’t possible. However, when we called back as two separate groups, we were suddenly able to secure two tables, despite being told earlier there was no space.

It left us feeling a bit put off. Decent drinks, but the experience and flexibility didn’t match the quality of other bars in the city.

Our favourite drinks were:
Skillability ver. 4
Moondust
Mehita

text.text:
I would give Moonrock a solid four-star rating. The bar is clearly inventive, especially when it comes to its cocktails. I ordered four drinks, and while each one was creative and had a fun, gimmicky flair, they didn’t quite hit the mark for me in terms of flavor. I tend to prefer drinks that prioritize taste over concept. That said, it’s easy to see why the bar has received numerous awards, and I can understand why others might find the experience exciting and memorable.
The food, however, was a pleasant surprise. It was well above average and genuinely excellent. If the drinks had matched the quality and deliciousness of the food, this would have easily been a five-star experience.

Very clean environment. Possibility to have locker for storing your personal belonging

text.text: Good clean hsr station. Has free shuttle service to tainan city center. Buy their bian dangs on second floor. Avoid the 2nd floor western restaurant. Its a rip off

text.text: Beautiful modern train station for the Taiwanese high speed rail. It is quite bit outside of Tainan city but you can reach the station from Tainan on the local train in about 20 minutes. The name of the stop is "Shalun" and from there it is a short walk to the HSR station. The station is super easy to navigate, even for foreigners. HSR trains are quite pricey but very comfortable.

text.text:
There are a couple of male employees working at the 後站 exit (probably in their 50s or 60s) who are extremely rude and entitled. Nothing bad ever happened to me, but I have witnessed more than one episode when they shouted at customers in an aggressive and rude way, just because they made a mistake or their card was not working.
They are really scary and their behavior is shameless. Why do you let them behave like this?
Oh and most of the other employees are there doing nothing, mainly checking their phones.

text.text: The owner is very friendly and puts great care into selecting books. Whether it’s the bookstore on the first floor or the reading area on the second floor, you can see the owner’s thoughtful attention to detail everywhere! Plus, the coffee made by the owner is absolutely delicious! There are also two adorable brother cats in the shop!

text.text: I found this place during a book sharing event. It's an independent bookstore with a unique style. The owner's care can be felt in the furnishings and small details. It's a good place to slow down, have a cup of coffee, and browse books.

originalText.text: 因新書分享會得知這裡；有個性的獨立書店，擺設及小細節都能感受到店主的用心。適合放慢腳步、喝杯咖啡、翻翻找書的地方

text.text: This small bookstore is hidden in the alley. In addition to new book recommendations, the owners also share their book collections. In addition to choosing books, you can also taste the desserts and drinks sold in the store. It is very suitable for a leisurely time alone.

originalText.text: 藏在巷弄裡的小書店，店內除了新書推薦，還有老闆們的一些藏書分享，除了來這邊選書，還能品嚐一下店家販售的甜點、飲料，非常適合一個人的悠閒時光。

text.text: The second-floor reading area is very cozy. I spent the entire afternoon there. Occasionally, the cat would come to read with me.
